content.topic_intro: Object Oriented Programming (OOP) is a programming style where programs are built using objects and classes.
content.why_learn_this: Most real-world Java applications use OOP because it makes programs organized, reusable, scalable, and easier to manage.

explanations.text: OOP helps create programs using objects.
A class is like a blueprint of a car, and an object is the actual car created from that blueprint.
OOP helps programmers model real-world things like students, cars, users, and games inside programs.
OOP organizes software using classes, objects, methods, and data abstraction.
Object Oriented Programming structures applications around interacting objects that encapsulate state and behavior, enabling modular and reusable software architecture.

car_example.explanation: All cars are objects created from Car blueprint.

student_example.explanation: Each student object has different data.

oop_benefits.title: Code Reusability
oop_benefits.explanation: Classes can be reused multiple times.

oop_benefits.title: Scalability
oop_benefits.explanation: Large applications become easier to manage.

mini_project.title: Student Management System
mini_project.task: Create Student class with name, age, marks, and display method. Create multiple student objects and print details.

revision_summary: OOP improves code organization and reusability.
